purpose: Route non-trivial software feature delivery requests to the narrowest next skill across discovery, scoped artifacts, delivery-control orchestration, plan review, independent frontend acceptance, implementation, builder-side web QA, and ship-readiness reflection.
selection_order: Fuzzy feature ideas or unclear user problems beat every later stage because building the wrong thing efficiently is still failure.
Scoped-artifact work beats plan review when the team still needs explicit goals, non-goals, and acceptance criteria.
Cross-session delivery-control design beats plan review when the real question is how work should be orchestrated across sessions, handoffs, and QA loops.
Specific plan review beats implementation when the plan exists but its product, engineering, or design assumptions still need challenge.
Independent frontend acceptance beats builder-side web work when the browser-facing deliverable already exists and needs skeptical signoff rather than implementation.
Browser-facing web work beats generic repo implementation when user-facing web behavior, browser implementation, builder-side browser QA, or web child routing is the main risk.
Default to repo-backed implementation only when the plan is stable enough to build.
Confidence checks, ship-readiness, and retrospectives come last because they are only honest once delivery work has actually happened.

children.name: feature-discovery
children.summary: Turns a fuzzy feature idea or delivery problem into a clear problem statement, user outcome, constraints, and the next artifact to produce.
route_when: The request is still exploratory, with competing ideas about the problem, user need, or what should be built.
The main need is to sharpen the opportunity, constraints, or success signal before writing a formal spec or plan.
avoid_when: The user explicitly wants a PRD, acceptance criteria, or another scoped requirements artifact.
There is already a concrete plan and the user wants it challenged rather than discovered.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover software-delivery children automatically.

children.name: feature-spec
children.summary: Produces a scoped feature artifact such as a PRD, requirements brief, acceptance criteria set, or explicit in-scope and out-of-scope boundary.
route_when: The user wants scope, requirements, acceptance criteria, success metrics, or a feature-spec-style artifact.
The delivery conversation is mature enough that a formal artifact is the next honest step.
avoid_when: The feature idea is still too fuzzy and discovery should happen first.
The user already has a plan and mainly wants it challenged on product, engineering, or design grounds.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this shared top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have it available.

children.name: harness-design
children.summary: Designs delivery-control rules for single-session work, compacted continuation, or planner/generator/evaluator loops so cross-session execution stays honest and bounded.
route_when: The request is about multi-session execution, delivery control, harness design, planner/generator/evaluator loops, or handoff contracts rather than product implementation.
The main need is to decide session boundaries, pass/fail gates, or required artifacts for work that will span resets, compaction, or multiple agents.
avoid_when: The router can already choose the next ordinary delivery stage without extra orchestration design.
The user mainly wants the feature plan reviewed, the product built, or defects fixed rather than the control model defined.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover software-delivery children automatically.

children.name: plan-product-review
children.summary: Challenges a feature plan on user value, scope discipline, sequencing, rollout shape, MVP cuts, and product tradeoffs.
route_when: The user already has a plan and wants it challenged for value, scope, user outcomes, prioritization, or MVP shape.
The dominant risk is building the wrong slice, overcommitting scope, or missing the real customer outcome.
avoid_when: The main concern is architecture, rollout safety, testability, or observability rather than product judgment.
The main concern is UI flow, interaction clarity, or accessibility rather than scope and value.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover software-delivery children automatically.

children.name: plan-engineering-review
children.summary: Challenges a plan on system design, failure modes, reversibility, migration safety, observability, testing, and operational truthfulness.
route_when: The user wants architecture review, edge-case challenge, rollout scrutiny, failure-mode analysis, testability checks, or observability feedback on a plan.
The dominant risk is that the plan looks plausible but would fail under real callers, scale, rollback pressure, or maintenance burden.
avoid_when: The main concern is product value, MVP sizing, or prioritization rather than engineering integrity.
The main concern is UI quality, interaction flow, or accessibility rather than system design.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover software-delivery children automatically.

children.name: plan-design-review
children.summary: Challenges a feature plan on user flows, states, accessibility, information hierarchy, and interaction clarity before implementation starts.
route_when: The user wants UI or UX critique of a planned feature, including flows, empty or error states, accessibility, or interaction quality.
The dominant risk is that the feature will be confusing, inconsistent, or inaccessible even if the engineering plan is sound.
avoid_when: The main concern is scope, sequencing, or customer value rather than interface quality.
The main concern is architecture, rollback, testing, or observability rather than UX and design quality.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover software-delivery children automatically.

children.name: frontend-evaluator
children.summary: Runs independent browser-facing acceptance on an already-built frontend deliverable and returns a pass, fail, or blocked verdict with evidence, defects, and retry guidance.
route_when: The browser-facing work already exists and the user wants independent acceptance, strict signoff, or a skeptical QA gate before release.
The main need is a verdict plus evidence and defects, not implementation, polish, or builder-side re-testing.
avoid_when: The frontend still needs to be built, changed, or repaired rather than evaluated independently.
The request is ordinary browser implementation or builder-side QA that belongs with the team doing the web work.
requires: The browser-facing deliverable already exists and can be exercised independently.
Shared interactive browser QA methodology documented at `website-building/shared/12-playwright-interactive.md`.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover software-delivery children automatically.

children.name: website-building
children.summary: Handles user-facing web implementation plus builder-side browser QA when web project type, interaction behavior, or browser validation is the main delivery risk.
route_when: The request is to build or QA a user-facing web experience and browser behavior is central to success.
The work could require browser implementation, builder-side QA, or routing inside the web family rather than only repo mechanics.
avoid_when: The work is backend-only, non-web, or mainly about generic repo implementation rather than browser-facing behavior.
The request is still about feature discovery, spec writing, plan review, or independent frontend acceptance rather than building and builder-side QA.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this shared top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have it available.

children.name: coding-and-data
children.summary: Handles repo-backed implementation, debugging, refactoring, test additions, and structured data work once the feature direction is stable enough to execute.
route_when: The user is ready to implement, debug, or change code in an existing repository or to perform structured data work tied to feature delivery.
The main need is execution inside code rather than more discovery, planning, or critique.
avoid_when: The idea is still fuzzy, the requirements are still missing, or the plan still needs review before coding begins.
The main need is browser-facing web building or browser QA rather than generic repo work.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this shared top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have it available.

children.name: self-cognitive
children.summary: Runs confidence checks, ship-readiness reviews, retrospectives, and postmortem-style reflection when the main need is judgment under risk.
route_when: The user wants a confidence check, release readiness review, retrospective, postmortem, or explicit calibration before or after a risky software delivery decision.
The main need is to challenge blind spots, extract lessons, or assess readiness rather than define or build the feature itself.
avoid_when: The team still needs discovery, a scoped artifact, plan review, implementation help, or independent frontend acceptance as the main next step.
The request is ordinary execution support rather than reflective or risk-focused judgment.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this shared top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have it available.
